passageOrStimulus: A bookstore says that its new reading lounge caused a 15% rise in sales last quarter.
stem: Which of the following, if true, most weakens the claim?

choices.text: Most customers said the lounge was comfortable.
The store began offering more staff recommendations at the same time.
A nearby book festival drew more visitors to all local shops during the same quarter.
The store expanded its children’s section.
Sales rose more for hardcover books than for paperbacks.

explanation: A general increase in traffic at neighboring businesses provides another plausible cause for the sales increase.

passageOrStimulus: A school claims that its laptop program improved grades because students can now study more efficiently.
stem: Which fact most weakens the claim?

choices.text: Teachers received training on the new laptops.
Students in the program were also placed in smaller classes.
Many students liked using the laptops.
The school library extended its hours.
The program was introduced at the start of a new grading period.

explanation: Smaller class sizes provide another plausible explanation for the improvement in grades.

passageOrStimulus: A city says a new bus lane reduced commute times because buses now move faster.
stem: Which fact most weakens the argument?

choices.text: The bus lane has clear markings.
The commute-time data were collected during school holidays, when traffic was lighter than usual.
Bus drivers were asked to stay on schedule.
Passengers reported fewer transfers.
The city also added new bus shelters.

explanation: If the data were collected during a period of low traffic, the reduction in travel time may stem from that rather than from the bus lane.

passageOrStimulus: A charity argues that its email campaign raised donations because the message was more persuasive than earlier appeals.
stem: Which fact most weakens the argument?

choices.text: Donations increased mainly from people who had donated in prior years.
The email subject line mentioned a deadline.
The charity redesigned its website that month.
The organization sent fewer emails overall.
The campaign included a short video.

explanation: If the increase comes primarily from existing donors, the message is not necessarily the main cause.

passageOrStimulus: A company claims its solar panels save money over time.
stem: Which fact most weakens the claim?

choices.text: The panels last more than ten years.
The company offers free installation.
Electricity prices rose last year.
The calculation of savings ignores maintenance costs.
Customers can finance the purchase.

explanation: If maintenance costs are excluded from the calculation, the estimate of savings is overly optimistic.

passageOrStimulus: A gym says a new app improved member retention because it made workouts easier to plan.
stem: Which fact most weakens the conclusion?

choices.text: The app includes calorie tracking.
Retention had already begun rising before the app was launched.
Many members downloaded the app.
The gym added a new class format.
Some users said scheduling was simpler.

explanation: If retention was already increasing before the launch, the rise cannot be attributed primarily to the app.

passageOrStimulus: A retailer says new packaging reduced waste because customers now return fewer damaged products.
stem: Which fact most weakens the claim?

choices.text: The packaging costs more to produce.
Returns decreased only for products that were shipped through a different carrier.
Customer surveys preferred the new design.
The company hired more warehouse staff.
Some waste was shifted to suppliers rather than eliminated overall.

explanation: If the waste was simply shifted to suppliers, the overall reduction has not been demonstrated.

passageOrStimulus: A manager concludes that remote work improved productivity because output increased after the policy changed.
stem: Which fact most weakens the conclusion?

choices.text: Employees spent less time commuting.
The company added more software tools.
The output measure was taken only for one team with unusually experienced staff.
Many employees preferred working from home.
The office was closed on Fridays.

explanation: A single atypical group does not allow the effect to be generalized to all employees.

passageOrStimulus: A training director says the new module reduced errors because staff learned procedures more clearly.
stem: Which fact most weakens the claim?

choices.text: Error reports were filed later than before, so the recorded number of errors fell even if actual errors did not.
The module included more examples.
Employees completed the module on time.
The trainer used simple language.
The company offered certificates to employees.

explanation: If errors are simply recorded later, the observed decrease may be artificial.

passageOrStimulus: A store owner says longer checkout lines are fine because customers value more product choices.
stem: Which fact most weakens the claim?

choices.text: The store added more product categories.
Customers spent more time browsing shelves.
The store offered a loyalty program.
Surveyed customers said they left because the checkout line was too long.
The store opened earlier in the day.

explanation: If customers are leaving because of the line, the argument that they willingly accept waiting falls apart.

passageOrStimulus: A city claims its bike-share program increased ridership because it made short trips easier.
stem: Which fact most weakens the claim?

choices.text: Ridership rose mostly in neighborhoods that recently gained new bike lanes.
The program included more stations downtown.
Many riders were new users.
The bikes were painted bright colors.
Some stations were near train stops.

explanation: Adding bike lanes in certain neighborhoods provides another plausible explanation for the increase.

passageOrStimulus: A company predicts that new eco-friendly packaging will increase sales because customers value sustainability.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the prediction?

choices.text: In a pilot test, sales in two stores rose by 8% after the new packaging was introduced.
The packaging costs slightly more to produce.
Competitors also advertise sustainability.
The packaging uses simpler graphics.
The product has been sold for many years.

explanation: A pilot test showing increased sales directly supports the prediction.

passageOrStimulus: A cafeteria expects demand for plant-based meals to rise next term.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the expectation?

choices.text: A survey found that 65% of students want more plant-based options.
The cafeteria changed its logo.
Some meals are cheaper than others.
The menu already includes salads.
The kitchen has limited storage.

explanation: A strong stated preference from students directly supports the expected increase in demand.

passageOrStimulus: A tutoring app is said to improve math scores.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the claim?

choices.text: Many students used the app at home.
The app includes colorful graphics.
The same students improved on a comparable standardized test after using the app.
Teachers also gave extra homework.
The app works on older phones.

explanation: An improvement on a comparable test after using the product is direct evidence.

passageOrStimulus: A museum claims its late hours attract more visitors.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the claim?

choices.text: The museum offers guided tours.
Attendance was highest on the late-night trial evenings.
The building has a new exhibit hall.
Some visitors came with friends.
Tickets are cheaper for members.

explanation: If attendance is highest during late evenings, the argument is directly supported.

passageOrStimulus: A subscription service claims that annual plans will reduce customer churn.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the claim?

choices.text: Annual plans are more expensive up front.
Many users prefer monthly billing.
The service has added new content recently.
Users who choose annual plans generally have the same age profile as monthly users.
Historically, customers on annual plans cancel less often than customers on monthly plans.

explanation: A track record showing fewer cancellations among annual subscribers directly supports the conclusion.

passageOrStimulus: A traffic engineer says new signal timing will reduce delays.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the claim?

choices.text: Travel times after implementation were lower on all three tested routes.
Drivers noticed the light sequence changed.
The city painted the poles a new color.
Traffic was heavy on one rainy day.
The old signals were outdated.

explanation: Lower travel times after implementation directly support the effectiveness of the new setting.

passageOrStimulus: A water filter is said to be effective.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the claim?

choices.text: The filter is easy to install.
It is sold with a long warranty.
The design is compact.
An independent laboratory found contaminants below detection after filtration.
The package includes a replacement cartridge.

explanation: An independent test showing no detectable contaminants is strong evidence of effectiveness.

passageOrStimulus: An office claims that adding plants improves morale.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the claim?

choices.text: Plants need occasional watering.
Teams with more plants reported higher morale in employee surveys.
The office has large windows.
Some employees dislike indoor decoration.
Plants were purchased in bulk.

explanation: An observed link between more plants and higher morale directly supports the argument.

passageOrStimulus: A company says its new onboarding module cuts training time.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the claim?

choices.text: The module is shorter than the old one.
Some trainees prefer video lessons.
The company hired more trainers.
The module includes quizzes.
The average time to independent work fell from 6 weeks to 4 weeks after the module was introduced.

explanation: A measured reduction in time to independence directly supports the conclusion.

passageOrStimulus: A retailer claims reusable cups save money because they last longer than disposable ones.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the claim?

choices.text: The cups come in several colors.
Break-even occurs after 3 months, and the cups last a year.
Customers like the feel of the cups.
The cups are slightly heavier.
The store added a loyalty discount.

explanation: If the break-even point is reached quickly and the cups last a long time, the total savings are strongly supported.

passageOrStimulus: A bookstore says an evening author event will increase sales.
stem: Which fact most strengthens the claim?

choices.text: The store has a large parking lot.
The author has published several books.
Members who attended similar events bought more books that week than comparable members who did not attend.
The event will include refreshments.
The store is near a train station.

explanation: Comparing similar customers demonstrates a sales effect associated with participation in the event.

passageOrStimulus: A restaurant says it can profit from lunch delivery because order volume is high.
stem: Which assumption is required for the argument?

choices.text: Customers prefer lunch to dinner.
The delivery fees and preparation costs do not eliminate the profit margin on each order.
The restaurant serves food every day.
Lunch orders are larger than breakfast orders.
Competitors also offer delivery.

explanation: For volume to be sufficient to generate profit, the margin per order must remain positive after costs.

passageOrStimulus: A company claims that switching to a four-day workweek will improve retention because employees will be happier.
stem: Which assumption is required?

choices.text: Employees prefer shorter lunch breaks.
Higher happiness will lead to fewer employees resigning.
The company can track resignations accurately.
Managers will continue to work five days.
The office is near public transit.

explanation: The link from happiness to retention must hold for the conclusion to stand.

passageOrStimulus: A factory installed automation that increased output, but profits fell.
stem: Which fact best resolves the apparent contradiction?

choices.text: Worker satisfaction improved.
The factory now produces more units of the same product.
The automation required a large loan with high interest payments.
The product is sold in multiple regions.
Customers liked the new packaging.

explanation: High financial charges can reduce profit even if output increases.

passageOrStimulus: A university is considering adding evening classes.
stem: Which question would be most useful to evaluate the proposal?

choices.text: Do evening classes use the same classrooms as morning classes?
Would enough students enroll to cover the added costs?
Do professors prefer teaching in the morning?
Are evening classes shorter than daytime classes?
Is the campus open on weekends?

explanation: Profitability depends directly on a sufficient enrollment level to cover the additional costs.

passageOrStimulus: A report says that neighborhoods with more tree cover recorded lower summer temperatures than neighborhoods with less tree cover.
stem: Which conclusion is best supported?

choices.text: Tree cover is the only factor affecting temperature.
All neighborhoods should plant exactly the same number of trees.
More tree cover is associated with cooler summer temperatures.
Temperatures were identical in all neighborhoods in spring.
Tree cover reduces humidity more than any other factor.

explanation: The report supports an association between more trees and lower temperatures.

passageOrStimulus: A firm plans to expand into a new city because demand for its product is strong there online.
stem: Which assumption is needed?

choices.text: Online customers in the city are younger than average.
The firm can hire enough staff and set up operations without prohibitive extra costs.
Competitors are absent from the city.
The city has a large airport.
The product is easy to ship.

explanation: The argument assumes that expansion is feasible at an acceptable cost and with sufficient staff.

passageOrStimulus: A study found that employees who commute less than 20 minutes miss work less often than employees with longer commutes.
stem: Which conclusion is best supported?

choices.text: Short commutes are one factor associated with fewer absences.
All employees should live near the office.
Long commutes never matter for attendance.
The study proves causation.
Employees prefer commuting by car.

explanation: The result supports an association, not absolute causation.

passageOrStimulus: A store had more visitors this month but sold fewer items than last month.
stem: Which fact best resolves the paradox?

choices.text: The store moved to a busier street.
The store closed for one day last month.
Many visitors came for a free event and left without shopping.
The store added a new display window.
Some items were discounted.

explanation: More visitors does not mean more purchases if a large portion comes for a free event.

passageOrStimulus: A company says that a new battery lasts longer because it has a lower discharge rate.
stem: Which assumption is required?

choices.text: The battery is sold in more colors.
The battery is not made of metal.
Lower discharge rate is the main reason the battery lasts longer in typical use.
The battery charges quickly.
Customers prefer smaller batteries.

explanation: The argument must link the proposed cause to actual lifespan under normal use.

passageOrStimulus: A city installed flood barriers and later reported fewer flood damages in the protected district than in unprotected districts during the same storm season.
stem: Which conclusion is best supported?

choices.text: Flood barriers never fail.
The protected district had no storms.
The barriers probably helped reduce flood damage in the protected district.
All districts should remove drainage systems.
The season was unusual because it never rained.

explanation: The comparison is consistent with the idea that the barriers reduced damage.

passageOrStimulus: A company claims that its new customer-support chatbot improved satisfaction because response times fell.
stem: Which fact most weakens the claim?

choices.text: The chatbot was available 24 hours a day.
The company also lowered the price of its products at the same time.
The chatbot answered simple questions very quickly.
Customers were asked to rate the service after receiving a discount.
The support team added more languages.

explanation: A price reduction can explain the improvement in satisfaction independently of the chatbot.
